required_tools.name: claude
required_tools.purpose: Claude CLI — primary interface for all automation exercises

required_tools.name: node
required_tools.purpose: Node.js LTS — runtime for Playwright MCP and npx-based tooling

required_tools.name: npm
required_tools.purpose: npm — bundled with Node 22.12.0; used to install Playwright MCP

required_tools.name: python
required_tools.purpose: Python — Zapier/API scripting exercises (Chapter 10) and utility scripts

required_tools.name: git
required_tools.purpose: Git — saving and versioning automation assets, skills, and CLAUDE.MD files

required_tools.name: curl
required_tools.purpose: curl — HTTP calls in API scripting exercises and Zapier webhook testing

required_tools.name: jq
required_tools.purpose: jq — JSON parsing in shell automation exercises and preflight verification

notes.audience: Tracy is a Senior PM, not a developer. The environment is intentionally lightweight — no compiled-language toolchains, no Docker-inside-Docker, no database servers. All exercises run in-browser or via Claude CLI.
notes.devcontainer_fallback: Tracy's primary environment is Windows 11. The devcontainer provides a Linux-based fallback for exercises that require a POSIX shell (preflight.sh, reset-env.sh). All exercises also have a native-Windows path documented in preflight.ps1.
notes.playwright_scope: Playwright MCP is installed as an npm package and invoked through the Claude CLI MCP mechanism, not run directly by Tracy. The install_check verifies the package is present so the MCP server can be registered.
